action.hypothesis: Severe stagnation and two non-competitive K=128 architecture probes suggest the current global-ranking support mechanisms are locally exhausted. A new `group_topk` prototype that enforces one local winner per small latent group before the global top-k may reduce redundant feature selection and improve reconstruction quality or cost under the same validated training regime.
action.summary: Prototype a `group_topk` SAE family in `sparsify/` and run it in proxy at K=128 with the current strongest gated training settings to test whether local competition yields a better Pareto direction than plain top-k, gated, or routed support selection.
action.self_review:

Local sanity check passed for `group_topk`: forward/backward succeeded and returned the expected `(batch, k)` latent activations and indices.
next_hypotheses: If `group_topk` proxy FVU is competitive with gated and better than top-k/routed, keep the family alive and stabilize the group size as the next family-specific axis.
If `group_topk` quality is mediocre but throughput is materially better, treat it as a cost-quality tradeoff family and compare at smaller K rather than discarding it immediately.
If `group_topk` is clearly worse on both quality and runtime, retire local-competition support selection and move to a more expressive multi-branch or factorized encoder family.
